element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players start each level with minimum pistol rounds only; ammunition not carried over between levels
Entered via marked doors; ammo counter displayed allowing resource sharing between two players
Redesigned from original as futuristic cyborgs; primary adversaries throughout
Both Leila and Albatross can only take two physical hits; bullet/projectile hit = immediate death
Player 1 (Leila, female) and Player 2 (Albatross, male) play simultaneously with identical abilities
Camouflaged Geldra base; establishes environmental variety and modern 1990s setting
Second camouflaged Geldra base; represents late-game difficulty escalation
Backup fire mode when ammunition exhausted; incentivizes ammo conservation

typical_relief_triggers: Entering marked doors for ammo/weapon upgrade
Reaching safe zones between enemy encounters
Two-player resource sharing (ammo counter in doors)
typical_tension_triggers: Ammo depletion forcing chaser bullet usage
Two-hit health constraint with projectile instant-death
Cyborg enemy density in later stages
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Wave-based arcade pacing with resource management tension; cooperative mode allows tactical relief through ammo sharing but maintains constant threat via strict health system

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Marked doors indicate ammo/weapon upgrade locations; players can choose when to enter

difficulty_curve.early_game: Florida seaside resort (Levels 1-4): Introduction to Masker cyborgs, weapon upgrades, cooperative mechanics; moderate enemy density with resource availability
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Transition between Florida and Egypt locations; escalating Masker encounter complexity
difficulty_curve.late_game: Egyptian pyramid (Levels 5-8): Camouflaged Geldra base; increased cyborg density and projectile threats; stricter resource scarcity with ammo not carrying over between levels
notable_spikes: Level 5 transition to pyramid location introduces environmental and enemy density shift
Final levels (7-8) likely feature Geldra organization climax encounters

notable_level_patterns.description: Game divides 8 levels into two 4-level location blocks (Florida resort stages 1-4, Egyptian pyramid stages 5-8), each representing a camouflaged Geldra base. Creates thematic progression and visual/enemy variety.

Marked doors serve as optional checkpoints where players can access submachine gun ammo. Ammo counter allows co-op resource negotiation. Pistol ammo resets each level, forcing relearning of weapon constraint.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All levels feature marked doors for upgrades

notable_level_patterns.description: Identical character abilities but different external appearances (Leila vs. Albatross, female vs. male agents). Both share the two-hit vulnerability and ammo-sharing mechanic, creating dependency-based cooperative pacing.
